footer.rights: © {year} Srang Tech Mai. Structure for what’s next.

hero.title: We build software, products, and ventures.
hero.subtitle: A small multidisciplinary team across finance, engineering, AI, and design.

mission.heading: <creativity>Creativity</creativity>, <engineering>engineering</engineering>, and <intelligence>intelligence</intelligence>; pulled into one system.
mission.lede: Not just a software house. A place where design, engineering, and identity are treated as one, held under structure and pressure until they become a single, living identity.

philosophy.heading: Structure for what’s <accent>next</accent>.
philosophy.lede: The ring is the environment in which new ideas land and take shape: foundations strong enough to support many products, many people, and many futures, without losing identity along the way.

takesForm.heading: From structure to <accent>reality</accent>.
takesForm.lede: What we build is never separate from how we think. Every system begins as structure.
takesForm.body: Then it’s pushed, tested, and refined until it holds under real use: technically and behaviorally, not just visually. We design identities that scale, tools that evolve, and systems that stay coherent as they grow, because what matters isn’t making something new, but ensuring it can keep becoming.

identity.title: Identity Systems
identity.desc: Living identities that adapt without losing clarity.

product.title: Product Systems
product.desc: Tools and platforms designed to evolve, not reset.

interaction.title: Interaction Systems
interaction.desc: Behavior-driven interfaces that respond, not just display.

identity.heading: Systems of people, not <accent>roles</accent>.
identity.lede: We don’t organize by rigid roles or departments. Small, high-context teams where design, engineering, and thinking live together, each owning outcomes, not handoffs.
identity.body: Every person carries their own generated ring, and works the way the system does: multi-disciplinary, high-agency, aligned but never boxed in.

products.heading: What we’re building.
products.lede: A small, growing set of products. Each one built the same way: structure first, then form.

items.members: A membership system for communities that want real structure. Identity, access, and belonging, in one coherent product.
items.cmission: Mission and task coordination for teams that move fast. Clear ownership, clear outcomes, no lost threads.
items.beacon: A signal layer for teams to find what matters, when it matters. Built for clarity under noise.
items.latent-write: A writing system that thinks in structure, not just words. For ideas that need to hold their shape.

cta.title: Ready to build something that lasts?
cta.body: Tell us what you’re working on. We move fast, think through details, and stay until the thing is right.

form.name: Name
form.messagePlaceholder: Describe what you’re working on, what you need, or what you’d like to explore.
form.successBody: Thanks — we’ll be in touch shortly.

showcase.heading: A showcase, <accent>in the making</accent>.
showcase.lede: Selected work, products, and the identities the engine generates will live here. Check back soon.

hero.nameLine: Every identity in the system is generated, not assigned.
hero.nameHint: Click the name on the badge to make it yours. Then grab the card and give it a swing.

opening.heading: Not roles. Not departments. Systems of <accent>people</accent>.
opening.lede: We don’t organize work by rigid roles. We build small, high-context teams where design, engineering, and thinking exist together, not in sequence.
opening.body: Each team is responsible for outcomes, not handoffs. Ideas don’t wait in queues. They evolve in place.

multidisciplinary.title: Multi-disciplinary by default
multidisciplinary.desc: Designers code. Engineers design. Not because they have to, but because understanding across fields produces better decisions.

smallTeams.title: Small, high-performance teams
smallTeams.desc: Teams stay small and focused. Less coordination, more clarity, faster iteration.

outcome.title: Outcome over role
outcome.desc: We don’t measure contribution by job title. We look at what was built, how well it works, and the thinking behind it.

ownership.title: Deep ownership
ownership.desc: If you touch it, you own it. From idea to execution: no passing responsibility.

craft.title: Craft matters
craft.desc: Details are not decoration. They are the difference between something that works and something that lasts.

align.heading: Aligned, not <accent>isolated</accent>.
align.lede: Teams operate independently, but not in isolation.
align.body: Across teams, we maintain shared layers of alignment:

design.title: Design syncs
design.desc: Keep visual and interaction quality consistent across teams.

engineering.title: Engineering syncs
engineering.desc: Align architecture and standards so the parts still fit.

system.title: System-level discussions
system.desc: Evolve the whole, not just the parts.

align.diagramAria: Three independent team pillars connected by shared horizontal alignment layers
align.closing: This keeps the system coherent, without slowing down individual teams.

fit.heading: Who <accent>thrives</accent> here.
fit.fitsHead: You’ll feel at home here if…
fit.notFitsHead: This might not be for you if…

fits.acrossDisciplines: You think across disciplines, not inside one
fits.lookAndWork: You care about both how things look and how they work
fits.figureOut: You’re comfortable figuring things out, not waiting for direction
fits.refine: You enjoy refining details, not just finishing tasks

notFits.definedRoles: You prefer clearly defined roles and boundaries
notFits.stepByStep: You rely on step-by-step direction to move forward
notFits.separateWork: You separate “design work” and “engineering work” strictly
notFits.speedOverQuality: You optimize for speed over quality and thought

closing.heading: We’re not hiring yet. But we’re <accent>building</accent>.
closing.lede: We’re still shaping the system. If this way of working resonates with you, you’ll likely find your way here when the time is right.

cta.title: Interested in our products?
cta.body: Reach out to learn about availability, how a product fits your team, or to start a conversation about what you’re building.

members.tagline: A loyalty system each business runs as its own.
body: Most loyalty solutions force a trade-off. Off-the-shelf tools look identical and bend awkwardly around how rewards actually work. Custom builds fit better, but take months and turn small changes into ongoing work. In both cases, the business adapts to the tool instead of the other way around.

This platform gives each business its own loyalty app, opened directly inside LINE — no download, no friction. Branding and operation live in one place. You define how it looks, publish changes instantly, and run the program day to day without relying on developers. The system stays consistent as it evolves, rather than drifting over time.

capabilities.title: Brand it yourself
capabilities.detail: Define colors, styling, and card design with a live preview.

capabilities.title: Run everything in one place
capabilities.detail: Points, rewards, missions, and member activity.

capabilities.title: Lives where customers already are
capabilities.detail: Opens inside LINE — nothing to install.

capabilities.title: Built for operation
capabilities.detail: Managed day to day, not treated as a project.

cmission.tagline: Mission and task coordination for teams that move fast.
body: Cmission treats a team’s work as a set of missions with a clear owner and a clear outcome, not an open-ended backlog. Tasks exist inside that mission’s context instead of floating free, so it’s always clear why a piece of work exists and what finishing it actually changes. Decisions and updates stay attached to the work they belong to, so nothing important gets lost in a separate chat history once a mission starts moving fast.

capabilities.title: Missions, not backlogs
capabilities.detail: Tasks live nested inside the mission they serve.

capabilities.title: One clear owner
capabilities.detail: Always visible on every mission, never ambiguous.

capabilities.title: Context stays put
capabilities.detail: Decisions and updates attach to the work, not a side chat.

capabilities.title: Built for speed
capabilities.detail: For fast-moving teams, not heavyweight process.

beacon.tagline: A signal layer for teams to find what matters, when it matters.
body: Every team channel eventually drowns in the same noise: updates, mentions, and alerts competing for attention with no way to tell which ones actually matter right now. Beacon sits above those channels as a signal layer, surfacing what needs a person’s attention and quietly holding back what doesn’t, instead of forwarding every notification at full volume. The goal isn’t fewer messages, it’s the right ones arriving with the right weight, so urgency stops being something a person has to guess at.

capabilities.title: Surfaces what matters
capabilities.detail: Priority rises automatically — no manual triage.

capabilities.title: Quiet by default
capabilities.detail: Loud only when something genuinely needs you.

capabilities.title: Sits above your tools
capabilities.detail: A layer over what you already use, not a replacement.

latent-write.tagline: A writing system that thinks in structure, not just words.
body: Most writing tools treat a document as a flat sequence of words, which is fine until an idea gets complicated enough to need real structure underneath it. Latent Write keeps the structure of an argument alongside the prose itself, so a long or technical piece of writing can be reorganised, checked for gaps, and built on without that structure quietly drifting out of sync with the words. Ideas that need to hold their shape over many drafts get to keep it.

capabilities.title: Structure with the prose
capabilities.detail: Tracked together, not structured then forgotten.

capabilities.title: Reorganise freely
capabilities.detail: Move an argument without losing the words.

capabilities.title: Made for long-form
capabilities.detail: Technical, sustained writing — not quick notes.

capabilities.title: Catch gaps early
capabilities.detail: Inconsistencies surface as you write, not after.

tools.heading: Generative tools
tools.lede: Small studio utilities built on the STM ring engine.

businessCard.name: Business card
businessCard.desc: Pick a ring, make it yours, spin it in 3D, and export a print-ready card.

form.title: Make it your own
form.sub: Your name and contacts print on the back — the site and QR are already built in.
form.caption: PDF · PNG · JPG · SVG — single card or print-ready sheet

trim.label: Trimmed
trim.hint: The bare card at 90 × 54 mm — for web, email, preview.

bleed.label: Bleed + marks
bleed.hint: One card with 3 mm bleed and corner crop marks — hand to a printer.

sheet.label: Print sheet
sheet.hint: Cards imposed on a press sheet with cut marks — give the file to a copy shop.
